title: Figure 1 from: Abuhaliema A, Gharaibeh L, Jaradat E, Hammad M, Alshaer W (2026) Non-coding RNAs in gene therapy: mechanisms, delivery strategies, and clinical translation. Pharmacia 73: 1-25. https://doi.org/10.3897/pharmacia.73.e173259
author: Abuhaliema, Ali
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 1 Classes of regulatory non-coding RNAs and their mechanisms of action. Schematic overview of major non-coding RNA classes—miRNA, siRNA, piRNA, lncRNA, and circRNA—highlighting their biogenesis pathways and principal mechanisms in post-transcriptional regulation, chromatin modulation, transposon silencing, and molecular scaffolding.
steps_raw: Figure 1 Classes of regulatory non-coding RNAs and their mechanisms of action. Schematic overview of major non-coding RNA classes—miRNA, siRNA, piRNA, lncRNA, and circRNA—highlighting their biogenesis pathways and principal mechanisms in post-transcriptional regulation, chromatin modulation, transposon silencing, and molecular scaffolding.
